llm_result.summary: The case describes rapid irritability to minor interpersonal triggers (e.g., dishes, tone), followed by post-episode emotional collapse and distress about self-behavior, with an explicit intention to be kind but difficulty enacting it. Core mechanisms to target include: (1) improved impulse control and prefrontal regulation during triggers; (2) stronger in-the-moment emotion regulation to prevent escalation; (3) reducing post-episode distress and ruminative self-criticism; (4) repairing interpersonal ruptures and aligning behavior with values; (5) enhancing self-compassion to bridge the gap between intent and action; (6) increasing adaptive mood-supportive activity (sleep, physical activity, social engagement) that supports regulation; and (7) leveraging social resources to dampen reactivity. The proposed levers span BIO, PSYCHO, and SOCIAL domains and include practical, changeable predictors with clear measures and short- to medium-term horizons. None of the recommendations rely on pharmacology or unsafe practices, and safety monitoring is included given the expressed distress. For acute risk, seek professional help immediately as needed.

solutions.solution_candidate: Establish a consistent sleep/wake schedule, optimize the sleep environment (dark/light, cool temp), limit caffeine after noon, and implement a 20–30 minute wind-down routine each evening. Track sleep duration/quality and daytime alertness.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Regular sleep supports prefrontal control and emotional regulation, reducing irritability and reactivity, and improving recovery from dysregulated episodes.
solutions.measurement: Sleep duration (hrs), perceived sleep quality (1–5), daytime alertness (1–5).
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns; monitor for excessive fatigue or sleep disturbances; seek professional help ifSI symptoms arise.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP_TO: C01,C03,C04,C05; DOMAIN: BIO; Sleep regularization.

solutions.solution_candidate: Teach and practice rapid regulation strategies to apply when triggers arise (e.g., box breathing, 4-7-8 breath, urge surfing, pause-respond-not-react). Build a brief trigger log with pre/post arousal ratings.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Immediate regulation reduces escalation in response to recurring cues, preserving relationship safety and personal control.
solutions.measurement: Arousal before/after trigger (0–10), frequency of snapping episodes per week, adherence to practice.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP_TO: C01,C02; DOMAIN: PSYCHO; In-the-moment regulation.

solutions.predictor_variable: Structured emotion regulation training (CBT/DBT skills) with daily practice (PSYCHO)
solutions.solution_candidate: Weekly skills session (emotion regulation, distress tolerance, cognitive reappraisal) plus daily 10-minute home practice and a coping-skills log; optionally couple-supported practice.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enhanced skills for modulating affect and behaviors reduces impulsive reactions, improves post-episode recovery, and aligns actions with values.
solutions.measurement: Daily practice adherence, DERS scores, mood variability (0–10), self-reported impulse control after episodes.
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns; monitor for frustration with new skills; escalate to therapy if needed.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP_TO: C01,C02,C03,C04,C05; DOMAIN: PSYCHO; Emotion regulation training.

solutions.predictor_variable: Post-episode repair script and partner communication protocol (SOCIAL)
solutions.solution_candidate: Implement a two-minute post-episode repair plan: acknowledge impact on partner, state intent to repair, request a short break if needed, and schedule a follow-up 24–48 hours later for a calm discussion.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Structured repair conversations rebuild trust, reduce ongoing hostility, and bridge the gap between intention and action.
solutions.measurement: Number of repair attempts, partner-rated warmth/trust (0–10), frequency of conflict escalation.
solutions.safety_notes: None beyond standard relational etiquette; ensure boundaries and safety for both partners.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP_TO: C03,C05,C07; DOMAIN: SOCIAL; Post-episode repair protocol.

solutions.solution_candidate: Daily brief self-compassion journaling and cognitive reframing after distressing moments; develop affirmations that align with values and kindness toward self.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reducing self-criticism and aligning actions with personal values fosters patience and the ability to act in line with kindness.
solutions.measurement: Self-Compassion Scale (brief), frequency of negative self-talk, mood after distress, perceived alignment with values.
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns; monitor for rumination or ruminative mood if overused.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP_TO: C04,C05; DOMAIN: PSYCHO; Self-compassion and cognitive reframing.

solutions.predictor_variable: Regular aerobic exercise to support mood regulation (BIO)
solutions.solution_candidate: Engage in 3x30 minutes of moderate aerobic activity per week (prefer outdoors, daylight exposure), with simple motion-tracking and mood check-ins.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Exercise improves executive function, reduces irritability, and stabilizes mood, aiding regulation during interpersonal stress.
solutions.measurement: Weekly exercise minutes, 0–10 mood rating post-exercise, incident frequency of irritability.
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure medical clearance if indicated; avoid overexertion; hydrate.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP_TO: C01,C03; DOMAIN: BIO; Exercise for mood regulation.

solutions.predictor_variable: Strengthened social support and partner-inclusive coping (SOCIAL)
solutions.solution_candidate: Identify 1–2 trusted support people and schedule regular emotional-support conversations; invite partner to participate in 1–2 coping-skills practices; reduce isolation during distress.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Social support buffers stress, reduces reliance on withdrawal, and improves relational safety and warmth.
solutions.measurement: Number of supportive contacts per week, Social Support Questionnaire score, frequency of withdrawal episodes.
solutions.safety_notes: Set healthy boundaries; watch for codependency dynamics.
solutions.mapping_query: MAP_TO: C02,C06,C07; DOMAIN: SOCIAL; Social support and coping network.
